howItWorks.intro: This opec production cut consumer price impact calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Oil price increase = (cut size / global supply) × price elasticity factor
Gas price increase = oil increase × 0.6 passthrough rate
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much oil does OPEC produce?
faq.answer: OPEC produces approximately 28-30 million barrels per day, about 30% of global supply. OPEC+ (including Russia and others) controls roughly 40% of global production.

faq.question: Why does OPEC cut production?
faq.answer: OPEC cuts production to increase oil prices when they fall below levels member nations need to balance their government budgets. Saudi Arabia needs roughly $80/barrel, while other members need $60-120.
